2604.11543.title: NovBench: Evaluating Large Language Models on Academic Paper Novelty Assessment
2604.11543.paper_summary: Novelty is a core requirement in academic publishing and a central focus of peer review, yet the growing volume of submissions has placed increasing pressure on human reviewers. While large language models (LLMs), including those fine-tuned on peer review data, have shown promise in generating review comments, the absence of a dedicated benchmark has limited systematic evaluation of their ability to assess research novelty. To address this gap, we introduce NovBench, the first large-scale benchmark designed to evaluate LLMs' capability to generate novelty evaluations in support of human peer review. NovBench comprises 1,684 paper-review pairs from a leading NLP conference, including novelty descriptions extracted from paper introductions and corresponding expert-written novelty evaluations. We focus on both sources because the introduction provides a standardized and explicit articulation of novelty claims, while expert-written novelty evaluations constitute one of the current gold standards of human judgment. Furthermore, we propose a four-dimensional evaluation framework (including Relevance, Correctness, Coverage, and Clarity) to assess the quality of LLM-generated novelty evaluations. Extensive experiments on both general and specialized LLMs under different prompting strategies reveal that current models exhibit limited understanding of scientific novelty, and that fine--tuned models often suffer from instruction-following deficiencies. These findings underscore the need for targeted fine-tuning strategies that jointly improve novelty comprehension and instruction adherence.
2604.11543.paper_summary_zh: 新穎性是學術出版的核心要求，也是同行評審的中心焦點，但不斷增長的提交量對人類評審者造成了越來越大的壓力。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs），包括那些經過同行評審數據微調的模型，在生成評審評論方面顯示出潛力，但缺乏專門的基準限制了對它們評估研究新穎性的能力的系統性評估。為了解決這一空白，我們推出了NovBench，這是第一個旨在評估LLMs生成新穎性評估能力以支持人類同行評審的大規模基準。NovBench包含來自一個領先的NLP會議的1,684對論文-評審，包括從論文引言中提取的新穎性描述和相應的專家撰寫的新穎性評估。我們關注這兩個來源，因為引言提供了對新穎性主張的標準化和明確的表述，而專家撰寫的新穎性評估則構成了當前人類判斷的黃金標準之一。此外，我們提出了一個四維評估框架（包括相關性、正確性、覆蓋範圍和清晰度）來評估LLM生成的新穎性評估的質量。對不同提示策略下的通用和專門LLMs進行的廣泛實驗顯示，目前的模型對科學新穎性的理解有限，且經過微調的模型往往存在遵循指令的缺陷。這些發現強調了需要針對性的微調策略，以共同改善新穎性理解和指令遵循。
2604.11543.author: Wenqing Wu et.al.
2604.11543.authors: Wenqing Wu, Yi Zhao, Yuzhuo Wang, Siyou Li, Juexi Shao, Yunfei Long, Chengzhi Zhang
